pubmed_links: [Koshimizu et al. (2021) BDNF predominantly regulates synapse-related genes in striatum; APOLD1 identified as top BDNF target gene](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/34529365/)
summary: APOLD1, a vascular early response gene regulating endothelial function and blood-brain barrier permeability, was identified as the most significantly upregulated gene in striatal neurons following BDNF stimulation, implicating it in BDNF-mediated neurodevelopmental signaling pathways; however, no direct genetic associations with autism, schizophrenia, or other neuropsychiatric disorders have been established. (novel candidate)
